::: {.r-fit-text}
## **🎯 Goal: Learn how to load `.pkml` model files in `{ospsuite}`**
:::

## Included Example

`{ospsuite}` comes with an example simulation of Aciclovir in a `.pkml` file. It can be accessed with:


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
system.file("extdata", "Aciclovir.pkml", package = "ospsuite")
```
:::


# Load

## Load a Model

Use the `loadSimulation()` function to load a `.pkml` file.


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
library(ospsuite)
simFilePath <- system.file("extdata", "Aciclovir.pkml", package = "ospsuite")

sim <- loadSimulation(simFilePath)

print(sim)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
<Simulation>
  • Name: Vergin 1995 IV
  • Source file: D:/GitHub
  repositories/ESQlabs/PAGE2026-R-WORKSHOP/renv/library/windows/R-4.6/x86_64-w64-mingw32/ospsuite/extdata/Aciclovir.pkml
```


:::
:::


# Inspect

## Attributes

`Simulation` attributes can be browsed using `$`.



::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
# Solver settings
sim$solver
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
<SolverSettings>
  • useJacobian: TRUE
  • h0: 1e-10
  • hMin: 0
  • hMax: 60
  • mxStep: 100000
  • relTol: 1e-05
  • absTol: 1e-10
```


:::
:::


. . . 


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
# Output paths
sim$outputSelections
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
<OutputSelections>
  • Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood):
  Drug, Observer
  • Organism|VenousBlood|Plasma|Aciclovir|Plasma Unbound: Drug, Observer
```


:::
:::



. . .


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
sim$outputSchema
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
<OutputSchema>

── Output intervals ──

<Interval>
  • Name: Simulation Interval 1
  • Start time: 0.00e+00 [min]
  • End time: 15.00 [min]
  • Resolution: 1.00 [pts/min]
<Interval>
  • Name: Simulation Interval 2
  • Start time: 15.00 [min]
  • End time: 1440.00 [min]
  • Resolution: 0.33 [pts/min]
<Interval>
  • Name: Simulation Interval 3
  • Start time: 120.00 [min]
  • End time: 1440.00 [min]
  • Resolution: 0.07 [pts/min]
```


:::
:::


## Simulation Tree

A `SimulationTree` can be generated using the `getSimulationTree()` function.

It will return a list of all the parameters available in the simulation.



::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
tree <- getSimulationTree(sim)

tree$Aciclovir[1:5]
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
$`Default Intestinal permeability (transcellular)`
$`Default Intestinal permeability (transcellular)`$path
[1] "Aciclovir|Default Intestinal permeability (transcellular)"


$`Intestinal permeability (paracellular)`
$`Intestinal permeability (paracellular)`$path
[1] "Aciclovir|Intestinal permeability (paracellular)"


$`Intestinal permeability (transcellular)`
$`Intestinal permeability (transcellular)`$path
[1] "Aciclovir|Intestinal permeability (transcellular)"


$`Mucosa permeability scale factor (transcellular)`
$`Mucosa permeability scale factor (transcellular)`$path
[1] "Aciclovir|Mucosa permeability scale factor (transcellular)"


$`P (blood cells->plasma)`
$`P (blood cells->plasma)`$path
[1] "Aciclovir|P (blood cells->plasma)"
```


:::
:::


---


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
tree$Organism[1:5]
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
$`SA proportionality factor`
$`SA proportionality factor`$path
[1] "Organism|SA proportionality factor"


$`Specific clearance (endosome)`
$`Specific clearance (endosome)`$path
[1] "Organism|Specific clearance (endosome)"


$`Surface area scaling exponent`
$`Surface area scaling exponent`$path
[1] "Organism|Surface area scaling exponent"


$`Volume (plasma)`
$`Volume (plasma)`$path
[1] "Organism|Volume (plasma)"


$Weight
$Weight$path
[1] "Organism|Weight"
```


:::
:::


# Export

## Save a Model

`Simulation` objects can be exported to a `.pkml` file using the `saveSimulation()` function.

This is especially useful after having modified the original simulation object.


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
saveSimulation(sim, "Aciclovir_modified.pkml")
```
:::


# Exercise

---

## Load a `.pkml` file

1. Load the example simulation object included in the `{ospsuite}` package.
  Remember, you get its path using `system.file("extdata", "Aciclovir.pkml", package = "ospsuite")`.


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code  code-fold="true" code-summary="Answer"}
simFilePath <- system.file("extdata", "Aciclovir.pkml", package = "ospsuite")

sim <- loadSimulation(simFilePath)
```
:::


---

## Parameter tree

1. Create the parameter tree for the simulation object
2. Explore the parameter tree within `Applications` to reach the `Volume` parameter in the simulation tree


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code  code-fold="true" code-summary="Answer"}
tree <- getSimulationTree(sim)

tree$Events$`IV 250mg 10min`$Application_1$Volume
```
:::

<script>
  // htmlwidgets need to know to resize themselves when slides are shown/hidden.
  // Fire the "slideenter" event (handled by htmlwidgets.js) when the current
  // slide changes (different for each slide format).
  (function () {
    // dispatch for htmlwidgets
    function fireSlideEnter() {
      const event = window.document.createEvent("Event");
      event.initEvent("slideenter", true, true);
      window.document.dispatchEvent(event);
    }

    function fireSlideChanged(previousSlide, currentSlide) {
      fireSlideEnter();

      // dispatch for shiny
      if (window.jQuery) {
        if (previousSlide) {
          window.jQuery(previousSlide).trigger("hidden");
        }
        if (currentSlide) {
          window.jQuery(currentSlide).trigger("shown");
        }
      }
    }

    // hookup for slidy
    if (window.w3c_slidy) {
      window.w3c_slidy.add_observer(function (slide_num) {
        // slide_num starts at position 1
        fireSlideChanged(null, w3c_slidy.slides[slide_num - 1]);
      });
    }

  })();
</script>
